title: Neil the seal swims out to sea, leaving joy in his wake
summary: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving behind a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.
imageAlt: Neil the seal swims out to sea, leaving joy in his wake
aiSummary: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving behind a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.
keyPoints: Neil the seal swims out to sea, leaving joy in his wake
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: Image depicting size of young seal with male adult seal with human adult for scale purposes.
multimedia.caption: Neil is expected to get much bigger as he ages. (ABC News: Hamed Akrami)

fullArticle:
Videos of Neil the seal have brought joy and laughter to millions of people around the world.

Experts say there's a reason why so many of us have fallen in love with this special seal.

Authorities say it appears Neil has returned to the sea after his latest visit.

The 1,000-kilogram marine mammal has spent the past few weeks at Seven Mile Beach, near Hobart, in southern Tasmania.

Neil the seal's antics have captivated people around the world. (Facebook: NRE Tas)

His bollard-bashing, fence-crushing, traffic cone-chewing antics have turned him into an internet sensation.

"Neil the seal is a global treasure," one fan posted on Instagram.

"The highlight of my day is seeing Neil content — it's what I live for," another devotee declared.
